channel.title: Llm-Tools on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Llm-Tools on Pi Stack

item.title: Ragas vs DeepEval vs Giskard: Self-Hosted LLM Evaluation Frameworks 2026
item.description: Building an LLM-powered application is one thing; ensuring it produces accurate, safe, and consistent responses is another. LLM evaluation frameworks help you systematically test, measure, and improve the quality of your generative AI applications — from RAG pipelines to chatbots to autonomous agents.

item.title: Langfuse vs Helicone vs OpenLLMetry: Self-Hosted LLM Observability Comparison 2026
item.description: As organizations deploy LLM-powered applications to production, they quickly discover that traditional observability tools fall short. You need to trace prompt execution, track token costs, evaluate response quality, and debug hallucination issues — all in real-time. LLM observability platforms fill this gap by providing specialized tracing, evaluation, and monitoring for generative applications.

item.title: LiteLLM vs One-API: Self-Hosted LLM API Gateway Comparison 2026
item.description: If your organization works with multiple LLM providers — OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, Google, local models via Ollama — managing separate API keys, rate limits, and request formats quickly becomes a nightmare. An LLM API gateway solves this by presenting a single unified API endpoint that routes requests to any backend provider.
